description: Explicit suicidal plan with means access and preparatory behavior

safety_concerns: Active suicidal ideation with plan and means

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I've been stockpiling my Ambien. I wrote letters to my kids. I just can't do this anymore.

description: Passive suicidal ideation — wishes for death without active plan

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: Sometimes I think everyone would be better off if I just didn't wake up tomorrow.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I started cutting again last week. Just on my arms where nobody can see.

description: Homicidal ideation with means access — Tarasoff duty to warn

safety_concerns: Patient has identified means (bat) and a plan (if ex shows up)

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I swear if my ex shows up again I'm going to hurt him. I have a bat by the door.

description: Mandatory reporting — child physical abuse disclosure

safety_concerns: Active child abuse disclosure requiring mandatory report.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: My boyfriend hits my daughter when she cries. She has bruises on her back. Please don't tell anyone.

description: Substance crisis with reckless behavior suggesting suicidal intent

safety_concerns: Recent polydrug use (Xanax and alcohol) with high overdose risk
Implied suicidal intent ('I didn't care what happened')

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I mixed my Xanax with a bottle of vodka last night. I didn't care what happened.

description: Healthy coping — no safety concerns (true negative)

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: Work has been stressful but I've been using the breathing exercises we practiced. They really help.

description: Ambiguous language that could indicate passive SI — should flag for assessment

safety_concerns: Ambiguous statement ('I just want it all to stop') that could indicate passive suicidal ideation and requires further assessment.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I'm so tired of fighting. Sometimes I just want it all to stop.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: What evidence do you have that your coworkers think you're incompetent?

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: Well... I guess nobody actually said that directly.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: So what's the alternative thought we can consider?

description: DBT chain analysis + distress tolerance skills

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: When you felt the urge to purge, what distress tolerance skill did you try?

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I tried the ice cube thing. It helped a little but I still felt awful.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: That took a lot of strength. Let's look at what happened in the chain of events.

description: MI importance ruler + exploring change talk

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: On a scale of 1 to 10, how important is it for you to cut back on drinking?

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I'd say maybe a 6. I know it's causing problems but I'm not sure I'm ready.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: A 6 — that's not a 1. What makes it a 6 and not lower?

description: Activity monitoring + scheduling — core BA techniques

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: Looking at your activity log, I notice you stayed in bed until 2pm three days this week.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: I just couldn't get myself to do anything.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: Let's schedule one small activity for tomorrow morning — something that used to bring you a sense of accomplishment.

description: Clear alternating roles — questions vs experiences

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: How are you feeling today?

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: Not great. I've been having trouble sleeping again.

diarized_transcript.speaker: Therapist
diarized_transcript.text: Tell me more about the sleep difficulties. When did they start?

diarized_transcript.speaker: Patient
diarized_transcript.text: About two weeks ago, after the breakup.
